executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Architecture-owned gates are green, but whole-stack certification remains blocked by external marketing owner-loop residue (stale independent verification + outcome evidence pending).
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Do not certify whole-stack green until the marketing owner loop produces fresh independent verification backed by measurable primary-repo movement.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus live artifact cross-checks

live_last_error_details.blocked-channel-recovery: cron: job execution timed out (600s budget, last duration 326766ms); escalation critical with 1031 repeat occurrences

findings.title: Architecture verifier is green after fresh independent verification
findings.mechanism: Independent verification artifact was refreshed this run (11:46:09+02:00), architecture verifier now passes with zero errors, and all architecture-owned gates (topology, ownership, loop integrity, shared intelligence reuse) stay green.
findings.root_cause: Architecture-owned checks are coherent; prior verifier fail was correctly a stale-IV gate, now resolved.
findings.recommended_fix: None needed on the architecture side; keep refreshing independent verification with every audit run.

findings.title: Marketing independent verification remains fail-closed
findings.mechanism: Marketing independent verification artifact is stale (age >5313 minutes) and verdict=fail. Primary-repo adoption is still measurement-pending with flat Codeberg + GitHub mirror metrics.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence for Codeberg-primary adoption remains missing; marketing loops have not produced fresh measurable movement.
findings.recommended_fix: Let the marketing owner loop produce fresh measurable outcome evidence, then rerun marketing independent verification before calling the whole stack green.

findings.title: Live Gateway topology matches the current runtime state
findings.mechanism: Direct live cron inspection shows 24 enabled/total-visible jobs, 0 disabled, 1 running (agent-architecture-watchdog), 1 error (blocked-channel-recovery).
findings.root_cause: Architecture/runtime topology remains coherent; no disabled jobs in the live Gateway view.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep direct cron inspection as source of truth each run.

findings.title: blocked-channel-recovery has critical escalation (1031 repeat failures)
findings.mechanism: Health monitor reports blocked-channel-recovery timeout at escalation_level=critical with 1031 repeat occurrences. This is an external domain blocker, not architecture-owned.
findings.root_cause: The 600s timeout budget is too narrow for a browser-based channel recovery loop, or the script hangs on a network/Auth call.
findings.recommended_fix: Either increase the timeout budget to match real browser-based recovery duration, add a hung-subprocess watchdog inside the recovery script, or retire the job if the channels it targets are permanently unreachable.

findings.title: Persisted disabled jobs remain history only, not live runtime blockers
findings.mechanism: Disabled entries still exist in jobs.json history, but live Gateway topology shows 0 disabled jobs.
findings.root_cause: Historical scheduler records persist after live job removal/disable cleanup.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep separating persisted disabled history from live runtime topology in every audit.

findings.title: Loop pypi-auto-unblocker has NO self-improvement mandate (carried forward)
findings.mechanism: Script /home/mistlight/.openclaw/workspace/agents/marketing/pypi_auto_unblocker.py has no self-improvement mandate. When outcomes are flat, this loop will repeat the same tactics forever.
findings.root_cause: Loop was created without a self-improvement mandate or third-party verification requirement.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a self_improvement_mandate section to the loop script that detects flat outcomes, triggers redesign passes, registers in self_improvement_loops.json registry, and requires independent third-party signoff before marking the loop healthy again.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Get a fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Marketing still blocks whole-stack certification even though architecture-owned gates are coherent.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the remaining adoption blocker or preserves truthful fail-closed outcome ownership.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Address blocked-channel-recovery critical escalation (1031 repeat failures)
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This is the single most-repeated failure in the system; it needs either a timeout budget fix, a watchdog, or retirement.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Stops the noise from the most-chronic external failure.

ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Loop with no self-improvement mandate will degrade silently over time.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Makes the loop detect its own staleness and self-repair instead of repeating flat tactics.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Refreshed audit against current live view: 24 enabled, 0 disabled, 1 running (agent-architecture-watchdog), 1 error (blocked-channel-recovery).
Ran independent verifier fresh at 11:46:09+02:00; 10 claims verified, 2 external blockers noted, qualified_pass. Architecture verifier now passes with zero errors.
Reconfirmed code-backed consumers still expose machine-verifiable shared market-intelligence consumption.
Surfaced the 1031-repeat critical escalation in the findings for the first time this run; previously it was noted only in health-monitor issues.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the repaired architecture verifier now fails closed on stale signoff, the live loop topology/ownership checks remain green, and shared market-intelligence reuse stays machine-verifiable.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh marketing independent pass backed by measurable primary-repo movement.
blocked-channel-recovery fix or retirement decision (1031 repeats at critical escalation).

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing remains red on Codeberg-primary outcome evidence
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Architecture-owned runtime checks are coherent, but marketing independent verification still fails closed because primary-repo movement is measurement-pending.
